item.title: DU takes progs to celebrate national poet’s birth anniv
item.description: The University of Dhaka has chalked out various programmes, celebrating the 127th anniversary of birth of national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam, on the campus of the university on Monday....

item.title: Thespian Ataur Rahman remembered
item.description: Independence Award and Ekushey Padak-winning thespian, Ataur Rahman, was remembered during a memorial programme at the Nilima Ibrahim auditorium of the Bangladesh Mahila Samity in the capital on Saturday....

item.title: BFUJ, DUJ demand reinstatement of four Ekhon TV journalists
item.description: Factions of the Bangladesh Federal Union of Journalists and the Dhaka Union of Journalists have demanded the reinstatement of four senior journalists of private television channel Ekhon TV, reports BSS....

item.title: Eid cattle market preparations in full swing
item.description: The preparations for cattle market are in full swing in different areas in Dhaka city ahead of Eid-ul-Azha, one of the biggest religious festivals of Muslims, to be celebrated in the country on May 28....

item.title: People-oriented system urged for ecology, economy
item.description: Speakers including politicians, researchers and activists on Saturday urged the adoption of a people-oriented river management framework that balances ecological preservation with economic productivity by treating the river as a living ecosystem......

item.title: BB unveils Tk 60,000cr loan to revive economy
item.description: Bangladesh Bank on Saturday unveiled a Tk 60,000 crore stimulus package to revive closed factories, expand agricultural production and restore business activities to support the troubled private sector, boost investment, and revive the economy......

item.title: Govt faces reform challenges amid uncertainty
item.description: Experts on Saturday said that the early months of the new government have generated expectations of change, although the reform agenda remains constrained by long-standing institutional weaknesses and ongoing geopolitical pressures......

item.title: Ansar-VDP members get Eid gifts
item.description: Bangladesh Ansar and Village Defence Party has organised an Eid gift distribution programme for its members and employees on the occasion of Eid-ul-Adha-2026....

item.title: International Tea Day celebrated at SMUCT
item.description: The Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology celebrated International Tea Day in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Depositors of 6 NBFIs demand full repayment this year
item.description: Depositors of six troubled non-bank financial institutions on Saturday urged a government order ensuring repayment of their full deposits, including accrued interest, by this year......

item.title: Tk 2,000cr paid-up capital must to pay cash dividends
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has made it mandatory for banks to maintain a minimum paid-up capital of Tk 2,000 crore from 2027 to distribute any cash dividends to shareholders......

item.title: German group plans expansion in Bangladesh
item.description: Germany-based global industrial thread manufacturer Amann Group is planning to expand its factory operations and explore new investment opportunities in Bangladesh, company officials said at a meeting with the Bangladesh Garment Manufacturers and Exporters Association......

item.title: BGB again foils BSF attempt to install poles
item.description: The Indian Border Security Force’s bid to install poles failed again on Friday evening at Tin Bigha corridor at Dahagram border point under Patgram upazila in Lalmonirhat after the Border Guard Bangladesh strongly protested against the attempt....

item.title: Shipping ministry bans cargo movement ahead of Eid
item.description: The Ministry of Shipping on Saturday issued a series of emergency directives to ensure safe and smooth movement of passengers on inland waterways during the Eid-ul-Azha travel rush, banning cargo transport on passenger vessels and halting all bulkhead movement across key river routes......

item.title: EU seeks to rebalance trade relationship with China
item.description: The EU must rebalance its trade relationship with China, the bloc’s industry chief said Friday, as Brussels renews its focus on commerce ties with Beijing......

item.title: Women drive Bangladesh economy amid inequality
item.description: Women’s labour is woven into every thread of Bangladesh’s economy- from garment factories and agricultural fields to tea gardens, construction sites and the IT sector. Yet they continue to face widespread discrimination, exploitation and deprivation in the workplace......

item.title: Modhumoti Bank holds 96th board meeting
item.description: The 96th meeting of the board of directors of Modhumoti Bank PLC was held on Saturday at the bank’s head office at Gulshan in Dhaka......

item.title: MIRPUR CHILD RAPE-MURDER: Charge sheet likely today
item.description: The police are expected to submit the charge sheet today in the case filed over the brutal rape and murder of an eight-year-old girl child whose body parts were recently found in a house in the capital’s Mirpur area....

item.title: Citizens Bank joins central bank schemes
item.description: Citizens Bank PLC has signed participation agreement with Bangladesh Bank to implement the Tk 3,000 crore Cluster Financing Scheme and Tk 2,000 crore agro processing for the development of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises......

item.title: Pragati Ins stands by Mashuk Helal
item.description: Pragati Insurance PLC recently provided a cheque to support the treatment of renowned Bangladeshi artist and painter Mashuk Helal......

item.title: Janata Bank holds ALCO meeting
item.description: The Asset-Liability Management Committee (ALCO) meeting of Janata Bank was held at the bank’s head office on Thursday under the chairmanship of managing director Md Mujibur Rahman......

item.title: Time for govt to take up tools to tackle crypto crime
item.description: CRYPTOCURRENCY, a digital or virtual asset secured by cryptography and typically built on decentralised blockchain technology, is as yet illegal in Bangladesh although it is not universally illegal. And it has become a headache as money is syphoned off in cryptocurrency through platforms dealing with the system. Cryptocurrency remains traceless, outside the purview of the......

item.title: Mutual respect, co-op must guide Dhaka-Delhi relations
item.description: The Bangladesh-India relationship, marked by unresolved disputes, once again appears to be entering a more difficult phase. After a decline in the bilateral relations after the fall of the Awami League regime, perceived to have survived on India’s unequivocal political backing, many hoped that Dhaka and New Delhi would rebuild bilateral ties on the basis of mutual......

item.title: Rethinking sexual violence and social justice
item.description: IN BANGLADESH today, conversations about sexual violence are often pulled in two sharply different directions. On one side are voices calling for a return to stricter moral codes, emphasising abstinence, discouraging co-education, and placing tighter control on women’s movement and behaviour. On the other side are those who argue for sexual autonomy......

item.title: Performative patriotism in social media
item.description: PERFORMATIVE patriotism on social media has become a significant social phenomenon in contemporary Bangladesh. It refers to the public display of national pride, loyalty or emotional attachment to the country, primarily for social recognition, online visibility, political alignment or symbolic identity rather than for genuine civic responsibility or constructive contribution......

item.title: Myth of saviour and forgotten memory of humanity
item.description: THERE is a haunting pattern that echoes through nearly every major religion, mythology and spiritual tradition humanity has ever created. Across continents separated by oceans and centuries, civilisations developed remarkably similar visions of the future: humanity would eventually enter an age of deep corruption, moral confusion, spiritual decay and social collapse......

item.title: My childhood friend, Tariq Ibrahim
item.description: MY DEAR childhood friend, Tariq Ibrahim (Kochi), passed away less than a week back on May 15 in Dhaka. I heard the news in Toronto from my daughter-in-law who heard it from Kochi’s daughter Manizey and almost immediately I wrote a few words to Manizey expressing my sorrow at the passing of my friend, her father. Even before I received Manizey’s request to write......

item.title: BRAC provides school bags to Ctg students
item.description: The BRAC Microfinance Programme in collaboration with the Guardian Life Insurance Limited under the Smart Students’ Finance Project has provided new school bags and educational materials to students at Railway Employees Girls’ High School......

item.title: Meherpur farmers eye Tk 55cr seasonal trade in lichi boom
item.description: LITCHI cultivation in Meherpur district is entering its peak harvest season, with farmers and traders expecting a strong market performance and seasonal trade worth up to Tk 55 crore this year, driven by expanded cultivation, favourable weather and rising national demand....

item.title: NCP, Chhatra Dal file cases over Jhenaidah violence
item.description: The National Citizen Party and Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal filed separate cases against each other following violent clashes surrounding the visit of NCP chief coordinator Nasiruddin Patwary in Jhenaidah, deepening political tension in the district....

item.title: Israel hits Lebanon despite ceasefire
item.description: Israel struck south Lebanon on Saturday despite a ceasefire as Hezbollah said its backer Tehran had pledged not to abandon the militant group....

item.title: Ukrainian strike on college in Russian-occupied town kills 18
item.description: The death toll from a Ukrainian strike on a college and its dormitory in a Russian-occupied town in eastern Ukraine has risen to 18, Russian officials said on Saturday....

item.title: Hajj pilgrim arrivals surpass 2025 despite war on Iran
item.description: Over 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia from outside the kingdom for the upcoming Hajj, according to a Saudi official, exceeding the number of international visitors last year despite the war in the Middle East....

item.title: Madrassah principal among 2 held over attempted rape
item.description: Two individuals have been arrested in separate incidents at Keraniganj and Savar in Dhaka on allegations of attempting to rape a minor girl, with both suspects later handed over to police after being beaten by locals....

item.title: Govt for strict regulation on use of hormones, fertilisers
item.description: State Minister for Food Md Abdul Bari at a seminar on Saturday said that the government would strictly regulate the use of hormones, fertilisers and insecticides, as their excessive use had become a major public health concern and was also causing financial losses....

item.title: Ex-Grameen Bank manager in Satkhira jailed over embezzlement
item.description: A Khulna court on Saturday sentenced Md Mokbul Hossain, a former manager of Grameen Bank’s Barodal branch under Assasuni in Satkhira, to five years of rigorous imprisonment for embezzling bank funds....

item.title: Ctg Dry Dock’s operational lease extended
item.description: The government on Saturday decided to extend the operational lease of the New Mooring Container Terminal to the Chittagong Dry Dock Limited for six more months or until the appointment of a new operator. ...

item.title: Over 40 injured as villagers clash at Chhatak
item.description: At least 40 people were injured in an-hour long clash between two groups of villagers in Chhatak upazila of Sunamganj on Saturday....

item.title: Woman killed by falling tree as tornado wreaks havoc in Sherpur
item.description: A 65-year-old woman was killed after a tree fell on her house during a sudden tornado that swept through Nalitabari in Sherpur on Saturday morning, causing widespread damage to houses and trees in several villages....

item.title: BGB rescues abducted contractor at Thanchi, kidnapper arrested
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh has rescued an abducted contractor during a drive at Thanchi in Bandarban and a kidnapper was arrested with Tk 3 lakh....

item.title: Youth shot dead in Khulna, body recovered
item.description: The police on Saturday recovered the bullet-riddled body of a youth at a fish enclosure in Khutirghat area under Daulatpur in Khulna....
